questions.prompt: Grammar — Present perfect: "I ___ already read that book, so I'll lend it to you."
questions.explanation: With I the present perfect auxiliary is have + participle: I have already read it. "has" is only for he/she/it, "am" doesn't form the present perfect, and "was" is the past simple of be.

questions.prompt: Spelling — Choose the correct -ing form: "The children were ___ in the pool all afternoon."
questions.explanation: swim has one short vowel + one consonant, so the m doubles before -ing: swimming. "swiming" keeps only one m, "swimeing" adds a wrong e, and "swimmming" has one m too many.

questions.prompt: Vocabulary — Which word is the odd one out?
questions.explanation: Doctor, nurse and dentist are all people who work in health, but a hospital is a place, so it is the odd one out. Sort by meaning: three are jobs, while hospital is where they work.

questions.prompt: Grammar — Obligation: "At my school we ___ wear a uniform every day; it's a strict rule."
questions.explanation: A strict rule is an obligation, and with we we use have to + base verb: we have to wear a uniform. "don't have to" would make it optional, "shouldn't" advises against it, and "have" alone (without to) doesn't express the rule.

questions.prompt:
Reading — Read: "Yesterday it was raining hard. Sami was waiting at the bus stop without an umbrella, so he got completely wet."
Why did Sami get wet?

options.text: He had no umbrella in the rain.

questions.explanation: The text says it was raining and he was waiting "without an umbrella", so he got wet from the rain (a). A reading answer must come from the text: a river, swimming and washing a car are never mentioned.

questions.prompt: Spelling — Choose the correct past participle: "We have ___ all the sandwiches; there are none left."
questions.explanation: eat is irregular: eat → ate (past) → eaten (participle). After have we use the participle: We have eaten them. "ate" is the past simple, "eated" is not a word, and "eat" is the base form.
